description_long: This object is a vintage radio receiver with a rectangular form factor and a wood-textured exterior casing. The front panel features a dark, glossy display area, a series of push buttons, rotary knobs, and a row of vertical sliders, likely for tuning and volume control. The top surface is flat with several rectangular ventilation slots, suggesting internal electronic components that require cooling. The rear panel is equipped with various input/output ports and labels, indicating connectivity options for audio and power. The sides are plain, continuing the wood grain finish, and the overall build appears robust and substantial. The design is characteristic of mid-to-late 20th-century home audio equipment, blending functional controls with a decorative wood finish.
description: A vintage radio receiver with a wood-textured casing, front control panel with buttons, knobs, and sliders, and a flat ventilated top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the control panel with buttons, sliders, knobs, and a dark display area.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain wood-textured surface and ventilation slots on the top.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing input/output ports, labels, and the continuation of the wood-textured casing.
description_view_3: Right side view with a plain wood-textured surface and visible ventilation slots on the top.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
